body.u: I left school at sixteen and a half with two O’levels and a few C.S.E.s. The job situation was a bit bleak, but not as bad as a few years earlier but I managed to get an apprenticeship as an electrician at a factory which made windscreens, this was alright for about three or four years. Then I left for a job a world wide firm which paid very good money.

Once I was settled at this new job, I got married to a girl I knew from my school days, she was also working at a well paid job a we was living well for a short time until we both decided to come down a peg or two and we ended up in a semi-detached house in Sheerness.

As the years went by a couple of kids appeared. My job came along well and I was offered a post out in Canada but with the kids around it was impossible so I had to refuse it, but now I wish I had not. I suppose the kids got have had just a good education out there.

I had been with this firm about seven years so I decided it was time to move on. I never really could stay at one job for very long. By now my wife had left her job to look after the kids. So my new job had to pay well. It was the same kind of job but it gave me less time off and I wasn’t very happy about this, but the pay gave us plenty to save up, but I worked out a swap with a workmate and this gave me plenty of time off to stay with the family. This was the job that I decided to stay with now until retirement.
